ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: A visualization suite primarily designed for single-cell RNA-sequencing data analysis applications but well-suited for other purposes as well. It introduces novel plots to represent two-variable and frequency data and optimizes some commonly used plotting options (e.g., correlation, network, density, alluvial and volcano plots) for ease of usage and flexibility.

A visualization suite primarily designed for single-cell RNA-sequencing data analysis applications but well-suited for other purposes as well. It introduces novel plots to represent two-variable and frequency data and optimizes some commonly used plotting options (e.g., correlation, network, density, alluvial and volcano plots) for ease of usage and flexibility.

name: r-henna
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '0.7.5' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-henna
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/henna_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/henna/henna_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 770c7754dfee12b984b2d0c5ad38be8a0d5f680965792f34fdff38ec5b194866

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-abdiv
    - r-dplyr
    - r-ggalluvial
    - r-ggeasy
    - r-ggforce
    - r-ggnewscale
    - r-ggplot2
    - r-ggraph
    - r-ggrepel
    - r-liver
    - r-paletteer
    - r-reshape2
    - r-rlang
    - r-tidygraph
    - r-viridis
    - r-withr
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-abdiv
    - r-dplyr
    - r-ggalluvial
    - r-ggeasy
    - r-ggforce
    - r-ggnewscale
    - r-ggplot2
    - r-ggraph
    - r-ggrepel
    - r-liver
    - r-paletteer
    - r-reshape2
    - r-rlang
    - r-tidygraph
    - r-viridis
    - r-withr

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('henna')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('henna')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://github.com/andrei-stoica26/henna
  license: MIT
  summary: A visualization suite primarily designed for single-cell RNA-sequencing data analysis
    applications but well-suited for other purposes as well. It introduces novel plots
    to represent two-variable and frequency data and optimizes some commonly used plotting
    options (e.g., correlation, network, density, alluvial and volcano plots) for ease
    of usage and flexibility.
  license_family: MIT
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/MIT'
    - LICENSE

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: henna
# Type: Package
# Title: A Versatile Visualization Suite
# Version: 0.7.5
# Authors@R: person('Andrei-Florian', 'Stoica', email='andreistoica@foxmail.com', role=c('aut','cre'), comment=c(ORCID='0000-0002-5253-0826'))
# Description: A visualization suite primarily designed for single-cell RNA-sequencing data analysis applications but well-suited for other purposes as well. It introduces novel plots to represent two-variable and frequency data and optimizes some commonly used plotting options (e.g., correlation, network, density, alluvial and volcano plots) for ease of usage and flexibility.
# License: MIT + file LICENSE
# Imports: abdiv, dplyr, ggalluvial, ggeasy, ggforce, ggraph, ggnewscale, ggplot2, ggrepel, grDevices, liver, methods, paletteer, reshape2, rlang, stats, tidygraph, viridis, withr
# Encoding: UTF-8

# Suggests: EnhancedVolcano, qs2, testthat (>= 3.0.0)
